text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-datepicker repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: when a `DatePicker` is rendered with `monthsShown={2}` and a `renderCustomHeader` render prop, the component internally tracks which of the two visible month panels the user most recently picked a day in. Once the user clicks a day inside the second (rightmost) panel, that tracked panel index gets stuck: if the user then uses the custom header's own month-jump control (i.e. calls the `changeMonth` callback that `renderCustomHeader` receives) to navigate to a different month, the newly selected month renders starting in the second panel instead of the first, and every subsequent `changeMonth` call from the custom header keeps landing in the wrong panel until the component remounts or a day is clicked back in the first panel. Expected: calling the custom header's `changeMonth` callback should always place the target month in the first (leftmost) panel, regardless of which panel the user's most recent day-selection came from.

Environment notes: package manager is yarn (yarn@4.9.2, already installed via corepack). Run a single test file with `node_modules/.bin/jest <path-to-file>` (or `yarn test <path-to-file>`). Fix the library source; do not just add workarounds in test files.

text: The relevant state is `monthSelectedIn`: `DatePicker` updates it from day selection, while `Calendar` uses it to offset the visible month range. The likely defect is that custom-header `changeMonth` reuses the same navigation path without resetting that selection offset; I’m checking the exact contracts and existing test helpers before patching.

text: The state ownership confirms the fix belongs at the library boundary: `Calendar` owns the custom header callback, but `DatePicker` owns `monthSelectedIn`. I’ll add a narrow internal callback used only by `Calendar.changeMonth`, have `DatePicker` reset the panel index when that callback fires, and cover the actual click-then-jump sequence in `datepicker_test.test.tsx`.

input.title: react-datepicker custom header month jump resets visible panel
input.content: In react-datepicker, monthsShown multi-month rendering uses DatePicker state monthSelectedIn to offset the visible month panels after a day is selected. A day selected in the right panel leaves monthSelectedIn=1; Calendar's custom-header changeMonth callback must reset that index to 0 before/while navigating so the target month starts in the leftmost panel. Implemented via an internal onCustomMonthChange callback from Calendar.changeMonth to DatePicker.

content.text: {"id":"252d921e-b0d4-4aeb-bb83-408026005956","title":"react-datepicker custom header month jump resets visible panel","assigned_labels":["react-datepicker","bug-fix","multi-month"],"status":"created","action":"created","importance":0.8}

text:
Fixed the bug.

`Calendar.changeMonth` now resets `DatePicker.monthSelectedIn` to `0` through an internal callback, ensuring custom-header month jumps always start in the leftmost panel.

Added regression coverage in [datepicker_test.test.tsx](/home/user/workspace/src/test/datepicker_test.test.tsx:2844).
